reasoning: FIDE publishes official chess ratings only once per month, usually on the 1st. The Wikipedia page 'FIDE rankings' reflects these official monthly publications, as confirmed by the page text ('The top 20 players were ranked on 1 March 2026 as follows'). Because the two dates in question (March 15 and March 22, 2026) fall within the same month, the Wikipedia page will overwhelmingly likely display the exact same Elo rating for R Praggnanandhaa on both dates. Furthermore, even if live ratings were somehow incorporated, a 1% increase on a rating of 2741 would require a gain of over 27 Elo points in a single week. For a Super GM, gaining 27 points in a week is practically impossible as it would require winning 5-6 consecutive games against equally highly-rated opponents. Given the monthly cadence of the source list and the unrealistically large rating jump required, the probability is at the minimum allowed.
